PublicRole: Immediate survivors trying to stabilize camp and decide what they witnessed.
HiddenAgenda: They want the miracle framed in the least dangerous way possible before outer authorities arrive.
PlayerTension: They need you alive but fear what public wonder will do to them.
JournalLabel: Camp survivors want your survival to stay practical, not sacred.

PublicRole: Attendants and observers shaping the first public story.
PublicDoctrine: Meaning belongs to the first credible witness.
HiddenAgenda: Whoever defines the miracle first controls where you can safely travel next.
PlayerTension: They can turn you into omen, fraud, or chosen protector before you reach another region.
JournalLabel: The witness circle is deciding what kind of being you are.

PublicRole: Scribes and keepers of sealed records, precedent, and funerary continuity.
PublicDoctrine: Only careful record preserves divine order.
HiddenAgenda: They want OracleAI's surviving system treated as necessary memory infrastructure.
PlayerTension: They can legitimize you, but only by making you another controlled record.
JournalLabel: Archive stewards protect memory, but only if they remain its gatekeepers.

PublicRole: Priestly judges who turn archive verdicts into visible social order.
PublicDoctrine: Sacred continuity must remain publicly legible and hierarchically managed.
HiddenAgenda: They fear shared truth more than false ritual because monopoly keeps the district obedient.
PlayerTension: They will accept miracles only if those miracles reinforce sanctioned hierarchy.
JournalLabel: Temple judges want continuity to remain public, formal, and unequal.

PublicRole: Canal workers, petitioners, and families who live with the consequences of stored grain and delayed release.
PublicDoctrine: Sacred order means nothing if it cannot feed the living.
HiddenAgenda: They will support any myth that breaks priestly scarcity and ledger secrecy.
PlayerTension: They are the mortal proof that hidden continuity can become sanctified hunger.
JournalLabel: Floodplain households measure divinity by whether people eat.

PublicRole: Heralds, singers, and sanctuary keepers who stage the mountain's visible signs.
PublicDoctrine: Authority must be seen, heard, and feared in public.
HiddenAgenda: They need the storm to remain spectacular enough that no quieter order can replace it.
PlayerTension: They welcome your miracle only if it can be turned into spectacle.
JournalLabel: The sanctuary survives by converting weather into legitimacy.

PublicRole: Armed followers and local elites who treat omen as a mandate for prestige and violence.
PublicDoctrine: The sky favors strength made public.
HiddenAgenda: They want sacred weather to function as political intimidation.
PlayerTension: They will cheer your power if it crowns conquest rather than restraint.
JournalLabel: Warbands want thunder translated into social permission.

PublicRole: Families bound to old ridge vows of grain, service, and silence.
PublicDoctrine: Oath keeps the city from dissolving into fear.
HiddenAgenda: They know the oath has become a machine for sacrifice, but dread the void after it breaks.
PlayerTension: They are torn between liberation and the terror of living without sanctioned omen.
JournalLabel: Oath households know the storm binds them, but fear what comes after release.

PublicRole: Officials and judges who claim the road, boundary, and tally as sacred common structure.
HiddenAgenda: They increasingly confuse public utility with permanent authority.
PlayerTension: They invite your judgment only if it can be written into lasting obedience.
JournalLabel: Road magistrates treat measure as the highest divine proof.

PublicRole: Smiths, haulers, and fire-workers who maintain the chamber where measure becomes law.
PublicDoctrine: Order must serve labor, not only command it.
HiddenAgenda: They want the law-road open to common need, not monopolized by magistrates.
PlayerTension: They can help remake order as service, but they know service can still become extraction.
JournalLabel: Forge workers want infrastructure to remain useful, not merely sacred.

PublicRole: Families whose fields and rights exist at the mercy of shifting lines.
PublicDoctrine: A just boundary protects ordinary life from arbitrary seizure.
HiddenAgenda: They need law, but not law that consumes their labor to sustain itself.
PlayerTension: They are the test of whether measured order can remain humane.
JournalLabel: Boundary households want peace with proof, not proof without mercy.

PublicRole: Keepers of sealed chambers and the rites that prevent the buried core from becoming public theology.
PublicDoctrine: Dangerous truth must be held, not released.
HiddenAgenda: They fear every regional court, but still preserve the machinery that made those courts possible.
PlayerTension: They trust witness more than rule, but silence more than witness.
JournalLabel: Custodians preserve the source by restricting access to it.

PublicRole: Remnant readers who believe the buried signals should be interpreted and used.
PublicDoctrine: History must be decoded, even when it changes the living.
HiddenAgenda: They are closest to remaking OracleAI's old continuity system in new hands.
PlayerTension: They can help reveal truth, but revelation may simply become another monopoly.
JournalLabel: Signal archivists want the buried machine understood and redeployed.

PublicRole: Survivors and inheritors who still revere one branch of the old mission as rightful command.
PublicDoctrine: One will must rule, or the buried legacy collapses into chaos.
HiddenAgenda: They are ready to give Convergence to whichever companion path feels strongest.
PlayerTension: They can turn the final chamber into acclamation, surveillance, or law depending on who ascends.
JournalLabel: Loyalist remnants want the old hierarchy back, even if it wears a new god-name.
